definition: Used to introduce a concession or statement.
Completely; totally.
As far as one knows or can see.
Find something out for certain; make sure of.
In the most fundamental respects; fundamentally.
Make a statement or situation less confused and more comprehensible.
In such a way as to allow easy and accurate perception or interpretation.
As a result.
Make an idea, impression, or feeling known or understandable to someone.
Arrive at a fact or a conclusion by reasoning.
Clearly show the existence or truth of something by giving proof or evidence.
Be a sign of; indicate.
Extending or moving from one place to another without changing direction.
Perceive or recognize something.
In a way that is readily distinguishable by the senses; clearly.
In such a manner as to achieve a desired result.
Develop or present a theory, policy, or system in detail.
Completely; solely.
Consider one thing to be the same as or equivalent to another.
Used to emphasize the basic, fundamental, or intrinsic nature of a person, thing, or situation.
Plainly or obviously; in a way that is clearly seen or understood.
Without discrepancy; used to emphasize the accuracy or truth of a statement.
In a clear and detailed manner, leaving no room for confusion or doubt.
Convey a thought or feeling in words or by gestures and conduct.
In a way that is concerned with what is actually the case rather than interpretations of or reactions to it.
In central or primary respects.
Draw special attention to.
Help to clarify or explain a subject or matter.
Explain or make something clear by using examples, charts, pictures, etc.
Make information known; communicate.
In a way that is not directly expressed; tacitly.
In a permanent, essential, or characteristic way.
At first.
Explain the meaning of information, words, or actions.
Show or prove to be right or reasonable.
In a literal manner or sense; exactly.
According to the rules of logic or formal argument.
Clear or obvious to the eye or mind.
Just; only.
As a necessary result; inevitably.
Especially; in particular.
In a way that is not influenced by personal feelings or opinions.
In a way that is easily perceived or understood; clearly.
Give a summary of.
To a higher degree than is usual or average.
Become aware or conscious of something; come to realize or understand.
Able to be perceived easily.
Virtually; almost.
In exact terms; without vagueness.
Mainly; for the most part.
For the most part; mainly.
Exclusively; solely.
In a logical or reasonable manner.
Identify someone or something from having encountered them before; know again.
Be an indication of.
In a way that is exact and clear; precisely.
In a way that involves rigid enforcement or that demands obedience.
According to the facts or exact meaning of something; strictly.
In most cases; usually.
